items.title: Sultan Nazrin launches Universiti Malaya Strategic Plan 2026-2030
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Shah today launched the Universiti Malaya (UM) Strategic Plan 2026-2030 at the university here. Sultan Nazrin, who is also UM chancellor, was welcomed at the Tunku Chancellor Hall by UM vice-chancellor Prof Datuk Seri Dr Noor Azuan Abu Osman. Also present were UM pro-chancellor Tan Sri Zainun Ali and UM board of directors chairperson Tan Sri Zarinah Anwar.

items.title: There are obstacles before success, says James Chai
items.summary: These were the words political analyst James Chai shared on social media last night before appearing at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today. Chai, a former aide to former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, is having his statement recorded as part of an investigation into a government-backed semiconductor project. Chai, who is based in London, had been sought by the MACC since March.

items.title: Students free to voice views, engage govt through proper channels - Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed student criticism and activism, saying that young voices are allowed to raise issues but should be channelled through proper engagement. Anwar was responding to Andi Mohd Asyraf Paturusi, 21, a Mara-sponsored student from Sabah and student Council representative, studying Bachelor of Computer Engineering at UCSI who noted that the landscape of student activism is no longer confined to physical spaces but is now driven largely online. "Student activism has changed over time particularly with the rise of social media" he said.

items.title: Zahid to meet Anwar today over Negri Sembilan political crisis
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi will meet Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today to discuss the political crisis in Negri Sembilan. He said he had an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday, and Tuanku Muhriz had decreed that the state government should continue operating Yesterday, Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said he would continue his duties as usual, adding that the state administration would function as normal in line with the decree of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir. Earlier yesterday, the state's 14 Barisan Nasional state assemblymen yesterday announced they had withdrawn support for Aminuddin.

items.title: Don't let universities become 'certificate factories' - Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on the Higher Education Ministry to monitor the quality of universities, warning that higher education institutions should not become mere 'certificate factories' producing low-quality qualifications. "I urge the Higher Education Ministry to monitor and safeguard university quality. Also present at the event were Higher Education Deputy Minister Adam Adli Abd Halim and Anwar's wife, Bandar Tun Razak Member of Parliament Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail.

items.title: Malaysia property market holds steady in Q1 2026 amid global headwinds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's property market maintained steady momentum across the residential, office, logistics and data centre segments in the first quarter of 2026 (Q1 2026), despite growing global uncertainty and rising cost pressures. She noted that the sector's strength lies in its diverse demand drivers, with residential remaining the largest contributor, office demand shifting towards higher-quality spaces, industrial assets benefiting from supply chain diversification, and data centres emerging as the fastest-growing segment. Residential, which accounts for 62 per cent of total property transactions in Malaysia, remained stable in early 2026, with transaction volume declining by 1.5 per cent but transaction value rising 1.3 per cent, indicating a more selective but still liquid market.

items.title: China pledges to strengthen energy security, economic self-sufficiency
items.summary: BEIJING: China's top leadership on Tuesday pledged to strengthen the nation's energy security and respond to external shocks by continuing to pursue a policy of rapid technological development and greater control over supply chains to strengthen economic self-sufficiency. The Xinhua readout did not mention the US-Israeli war on Iran, but said: "We must systematically respond to external shocks and challenges, improve energy resource security guarantee levels and counter various uncertainties with the certainty of high-quality development." China's economy grew 5.0 per cent in the first quarter, at the top of its full-year target range of 4.5 per cent to 5.0 per cent, showing higher resilience than most other economies to the energy and commodity shocks caused by the Iran war.

items.title: Unity government unaffected by political developments in NS, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Negri Sembilan crisis does not affect the unity government at the federal level, government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. Fahmi, who is also the Communications Minister, said the government remains concerned about the political developments in the state. Meanwhile, when asked about a meeting between Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also PH chairman, and Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Fahmi said he had no information.

items.title: Former Bar president calls for 'Charity Commission' amid proposed new MACC laws
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The regulatory framework governing charitable foundations requires a radical overhaul, says former Malaysian Bar president Salim Bashir. His call aligns with ongoing discussions regarding the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission's proposed new legislative package, which includes a Political Financing Act, a Welfare Act, and a Public Office Misconduct Act aimed at tightening financial governance. To bridge persistent systemic gaps in oversight, Salim advocated for the creation of a statutory Charity Commission to serve as a dedicated regulatory body for the sector.

items.title: Indonesia to remove import duties on some plastic products and LPG for petrochemical industry
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia plans to remove import duties on some plastic products as well as liquified petroleum gas (LPG) bought by the petrochemical industry to help plastic producers cope with a naphtha shortage, its chief economic minister said on Tuesday. He said the import duty will be cut to zero per cent on materials used to make plastic packaging, including polypropylene, LLDPE and High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE). "We will give everything zero per cent import duty but only for six months," he said.

items.title: Malaysia, EU in final stage of signing free trade agreement
items.summary: Mohamad said that during the meeting with Kallas, he also raised the issue of the strong anti-palm oil campaign in Europe and rejected the ongoing anti-palm oil campaigns being actively promoted in countries in the region. "I said that it is unfair because labelling food as containing palm oil clearly violates trade ethics," he said. Meanwhile, Mohamad said Malaysia's current focus is on exporting processed palm oil with higher added value, rather than merely exporting crude palm oil.

items.title: No deal after 30 years: Penang CM confirms talks failed before Waterfall Cafe demolition
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 28 — Talks between the Penang government and Waterfall Cafe have failed to produce a settlement, bringing to an end a decades-long dispute that has persisted since 1990, Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said today. “Despite outstanding rental arrears, the state government through the Northeast District and Land Office (PDTTL) offered several reasonable options to Waterfall Cafe to relocate and continue operations,” he said. He said the cafe owners rejected the offers without clear justification.

items.title: Top Jalisco cartel leader 'El Jardinero' arrested in Mexico
items.summary: MONTERREY: Mexican special forces have arrested Audias Flores, known as "El Jardinero" and one of the top commanders of the powerful Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), in the western state of Nayarit, security minister Omar Garcia Harfuch said on Monday. Flores – a regional commander in control of swathes of CJNG territory along Mexico's Pacific coast – was considered a potential successor to Nemesio Oseguera, alias "El Mencho," who ran the cartel and was killed in a security operation in February. It was not immediately clear if Flores would face charges in Mexico, but Garcia Harfuch said he was wanted for extradition by US authorities, who have offered a US$5 million reward for his arrest.

items.title: As scams hit RM2.9b, Fahmi warns of AI-powered fraud threats, calls for stronger action
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysians have lost about RM2.9 billion to online scams, a figure Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today described as a serious and escalating national threat. Speaking at the Google 2026 APAC Online Safety Dialogue, Fahmi said the scale of losses showed the urgent need for stronger coordinated action between the government, tech companies, and regional partners such as Asean. Fahmi praised Google’s efforts in tackling online fraud and said Malaysia is open to deeper collaboration with technology firms and the NSRC.

items.title: Johor Regent meets DPM Fadillah, MB to discuss water supply, energy sustainability
items.summary: JOHOR BARU, April 28 — Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Regent of Johor, received a courtesy call from Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Fadillah Yusof and state leaders today to discuss issues related to water supply and energy sustainability in the state. In a Facebook post today, the Johor Regent said the meeting involved the deputy prime minister, who is also energy transition and water transformation minister, Fadillah; Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi; and Johor State Secretary Datuk Mohammed Ridha Abd Kadir. Among the matters discussed were efforts to ensure a more systematic water supply system and sustainable electricity generation to guarantee sufficient resources for Johor’s future development needs.

items.title: LPI Capital Q1 profit edges up on stronger investment income
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: LPI Capital Bhd's net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, rose 1.6 per cent to RM99.5 million from RM97.98 million a year ago, supported by higher profit from the investment holding segment. According to a bourse filing, the investment holding segment's pre-tax profit jumped 10.8 per cent to RM23.6 million from RM21.3 million previously, mainly contributed by higher tax-exempt dividend income received from its equity investment. Revenue also grew six per cent to RM547.7 million from RM515 million last year, driven by the general insurance segment.

items.title: Matrade targets RM4.5bil in sales at Mihas 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) is targeting RM4.5 billion in sales at the Malaysia International Halal Showcase 2026 (Mihas 2026). He said Mihas 2026 will introduce a new component, Women in Export (WiEX), which combines entrepreneurial elements for women entrepreneurs and women-owned exporters to enable them to explore international markets. "It reflects Malaysia's inclusive growth model, where more capable businesses, especially women-led enterprises, are empowered to compete globally, driving greater exports of ideas, innovation and value-added products as part of Malaysia's next-generation trade strategy," he said.

items.title: Muhyiddin’s trial: Mamfor’s RM19.5m ‘donations’ in 29 cheques to Bersatu flagged as suspicious, unusual to give money to political party, banker says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Mamfor Sdn Bhd’s payment of RM19.5 million in frequent and large transactions in 2021 and 2022 as “donations” to Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia was unusual, and all 29 cheques used to pay the money was flagged internally as suspicious transactions, the High Court heard today. Bank Muamalat’s PKNS Shah Alam assistant branch manager Norazeanti Miswan said this while testifying as the ninth prosecution witness in Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s corruption and money laundering trial. Norazeanti said her bank branch had internally reported each of Mamfor’s 29 Bank Muamalat cheques, due to the “large and frequent withdrawals paid to” Bersatu.

items.title: Govt to introduce national laureate-led mentorship programme for young writers
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Education Ministry will introduce a mentorship programme led by national laureates to nurture young writers and strengthen the country's literary ecosystem. She said the programme is important as literature reflected humanity, nurtured sympathy and empathy, and recorded the values and wisdom of life as the foundation for building national civilisation. She said national laureates were writers whose works reflected strong ideas, social observation and national values.

items.title: Masidi: Sabah entitled to RM3.019bil from last year's income tax, Customs
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah is entitled to at least RM3.019 billion from federal revenue collected in the state last year, said Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun. The Sabah finance minister said, for 2025, total collections from direct taxes under the Inland Revenue Board and indirect taxes under the Customs Department are estimated at RM7.547 billion. "Based on this, Sabah's 40 per cent special grant entitlement amounts to approximately RM3.019 billion," he said in reply to questions during the Sabah Legislative Assembly sitting by Alias Sani (Warisan-Sekong) and Datuk Seri Jeffrey Kitingan (Star-Tambunan) on the exact amount Sabah is entitled to from federal revenue collected in the state.

items.title: Google signs classified AI deal with Pentagon, The Information reports
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Alphabet's Google joined a growing list of technology firms to sign a deal with the US Department of Defence to use its artificial intelligence models for classified work, The Information reported on Tuesday, citing a person familiar with the matter. The agreement allows the Pentagon to use Google's AI for "any lawful government purpose", the report added, putting it alongside OpenAI and Elon Musk's xAI, which also have to supply AI models for classified use. The Pentagon signed agreements worth up to US$200 million each with major AI labs in 2025, including Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google.

items.title: North Korea sharply increased executions during pandemic lockdown, rights group says
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea sharply raised the number of executions it conducted after shutting its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, with the largest share linked to violations of bans on foreign culture and religion, a Seoul-based civic group said on Monday. The findings were based on interviews with 880 North Korean defectors living in South Korea and the group used satellite images to map execution sites. The report said breaches of restrictions on foreign culture and religion, including watching South Korean dramas and movies, accounted for the largest portion of the executions.

items.title: Four suspected cable thieves injured in crash while fleeing police
items.summary: "At the scene, the fire and rescue team found that the accident involved two vehicles — a Toyota Hilux Vigo carrying four victims and an Isuzu Invader with one victim. "However, the vehicle crashed into the Isuzu Invader, causing all occupants in the Toyota Hilux and the sole occupant in the Isuzu Invader to sustain injuries. "The fire team extricated the elderly man who was trapped inside the Isuzu Invader using special equipment," he said.

items.title: LHDN ‘killing the golden goose’? Lim Guan Eng questions tax surge, urges aid for struggling MSMEs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Lim Guan Eng has questioned whether Malaysia’s rising tax collection is putting pressure on businesses, warning that overburdening companies could undermine long-term revenue growth. He argued that the jump in tax collection appears disproportionate to Malaysia’s economic growth, noting that gross domestic product (GDP) expanded by just 5.2 per cent in the same year. He warned that without thriving businesses generating income, the government’s ability to sustain tax revenue would be at risk.

items.title: Jalaluddin: BN's withdrawal of support not a 'backdoor' move
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen have denied that their withdrawal of support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun is a tebuk atap (backdoor) move to topple the state government. Pertang assemblyman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the decision was made to defend party principles and prevent political interference in matters concerning the monarchy and customary institutions. He said the move followed a crisis involving customary institutions and the state constitution, which the administration failed to resolve, leading to open debate on social media.

items.title: Malaysia submits response to US probe over forced labour, excess capacity claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysia has submitted an explanation to the United States (US) regarding allegations of structural excess capacity and production as well as the use of forced labour, which are among the issues being investigated under Section 301(b) of the US Trade Act of 1974. Minister of Investment, Trade and Industry Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said Malaysia has followed the law accordingly. Recently, the US, through US Trade Representative (USTR) Ambassador Jamieson Greer, launched investigations on 60 economies under Section 301(b) of the US Trade Act 1974, a section created, among other things, to address failures to take action against forced labour.

items.title: Nestlé Malaysia posts strong Q1 FY26 profit, hits highest quarterly earnings in four years
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd's net profit rose to RM205.12 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026, from RM161.34 million a year earlier. This marks its highest quarterly net profit since Q1 financial year 2022 (FY22), when it recorded RM205.18 million. The higher net profit was driven by solid topline growth, disciplined cost management and ongoing operational efficiencies across the value chain.

items.title: Anwar: N. Sembilan assembly dissolution ‘not an issue’ for now amid crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today speculation that the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly would be dissolved “is not an issue yet”. Anwar met the state’s Ruler Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Muniwar last night amid an unfolding political crisis that is threatening to break the Pakatan Harapan-led state administration. Today, he said Tuanku Muhriz had decreed that Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun would remain as menteri besar but was unclear when asked to comment on Umno’s claim that the party now has the simple majority to form a new government.

items.title: Negri Sembilan Umno exco members ordered to vacate offices, return state assets
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Umno has ordered its four state executive councillors to vacate their offices and return all government assets, including official vehicles. State Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the decision to return the assets to the state secretary today follows the unanimous withdrawal of support by 14 Umno/Barisan Nasional (BN) assemblymen for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun. "Today, I have directed all exco members representing Umno to surrender all facilities, including official vehicles, to the state government," he said here today.

items.title: ‘Scam Ready Asean’ programme hopes to boost digital safety and scam awareness
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — A new regional initiative, “Scam Ready Asean,” was officially launched today at the Google 2026 APAC Online Safety Dialogue event in Kuala Lumpur. The programme is designed to enhance digital safety and combat online scams across Southeast Asia, as cyber threats continue to rise alongside the region’s fast-growing digital economy. Backed by a US$5 million (RM19.7 million) contribution from Google, the initiative will combine scam prevention tools, public awareness efforts, and large-scale digital literacy training across Asean member states.

items.title: Train collision kills 14, injures dozens near Jakarta
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's president ordered an investigation Tuesday after a long-distance train smashed into a stationary commuter train overnight, killing 14 people and injuring dozens. Officials ended a nearly 12-hour rescue effort near Bekasi Timur station, east of the capital Jakarta, which saw crews prising open mangled carriages following the Monday night collision. "There were two announcements from the commuter train.

items.title: Salesman charged with attempting to murder ex-girlfriend
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: A building materials salesman was charged at the magistrate's court here today with attempting to murder his former girlfriend earlier this month. The offence was allegedly committed about 9.45pm along Jalan Lorong Perusahaan Maju 8, Auto City, Prai, on April 15. The court allowed bail at RM10,000 with one surety, along with an additional condition that the accused is not to disturb the victims or prosecution witnesses.

items.title: Umno denies Perikatan pact to topple N. Sembilan MB Aminuddin, insists no takeover deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Negeri Sembilan Umno has denied any conspiracy or prior arrangement with Perikatan Nasional (PN) to remove Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun, insisting that its withdrawal of support was based solely on loss of confidence in the PKR vice-president. Negeri Sembilan Umno chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the 14 Barisan Nasional assemblymen were not seeking to take over the state government, but had collectively lost confidence in Aminuddin’s leadership. Jalaluddin said BN had initially been prepared to sit in opposition if PN’s five assemblymen chose to continue backing the Pakatan Harapan-led government, but the situation changed when PN instead indicated willingness to support BN.

items.title: Malaysian Bar: Naming a new MACC chief no 'panacea' for institutional gaps
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Bar has welcomed the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner effective May 13. "The MACC must be reformed to fortify its independence through transparent appointments and a fixed term for its chief commissioner to avoid unnecessary annual speculation," said Malaysian Bar president Anand Raj. The Bar reiterated calls from its "Memorandum for the Reform of the MACC" to insulate the commission from executive influence and secure leadership tenure to ensure true institutional independence.

items.title: Anwar: Business as usual for N. Sembilan MB Aminuddin amid political turmoil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today he had instructed Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun to carry out his duties as per usual, even as the Negeri Sembilan Umno chapter had openly expressed its intention to form a new government. Umno has 14 of the 36-seated state legislative assembly. Yesterday, Negeri Sembilan Umno chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias declared all 14 Umno assemblymen had withdrawn their support for Aminuddin, citing loss of confidence because of the way the latter handled the royal dispute.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar to meet Zahid over Negri Sembilan political situation today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to meet Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today to discuss recent political developments in Negri Sembilan. Anwar confirmed the planned meeting when speaking to reporters, following an audience with the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, yesterday. He said Tuanku Muhriz had decreed that the state government should continue operating as usual amid the ongoing situation.

items.title: Zahid backs proposal to upgrade KPDN enforcement division into department
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 28 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has voiced support for the Enforcement Division of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) to be upgraded to a department to strengthen the division’s capacity in dealing with increasingly complex consumerism and enforcement issues. He said the role of the enforcement team is no longer limited to traditional enforcement, but rather encompasses domestic trade issues, subsidy leakage and cross-border smuggling involving various ministries and agencies. “The time has come for the Enforcement Division to be upgraded to the Enforcement Department.

items.title: Gombak Setia rep suspended two days for defying Selangor Speaker
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Gombak Setia assemblyman Muhammad Hilman Idham has been ordered out of the Selangor state assembly and suspended for two days starting today for defying Speaker Lau Weng San. Lau issued the order after Hilman ignored instructions to sit down following a reply by state Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin on potential state revenue from renewable energy. Lau then ordered Hilman to leave the chambers and suspended him until tomorrow.

items.title: Changing MACC chief not enough, wider reforms still needed, says Malaysian Bar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The Malaysian Bar has welcomed the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as Chief Commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), but said the leadership change alone is not sufficient to strengthen the country’s anti-corruption framework. In a statement today, the Bar said while it acknowledges the end of uncertainty over the MACC leadership transition, questions remain over whether current arrangements are adequate to ensure public confidence and institutional independence. The Bar reiterated its long-standing call for reforms to the MACC, including stronger institutional independence, transparent appointment processes and a fixed tenure for the chief commissioner to avoid recurring uncertainty and annual speculation.

items.title: N. Sembilan crisis to be settled through political negotiations, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The political crisis in Negeri Sembilan will be resolved through political means, Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today. Zahid said the party had never interfered in the administration of the state government or matters involving Negeri Sembilan’s adat laws, according to a report by Sinar Haria n today. BN state chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the decision followed a crisis involving the state’s adat institutions and constitutional matters.

items.title: [UPDATED] Aminuddin to stay on as Negri MB following royal decree – Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will continue serving as Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar following a decree by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar. Anwar said the state's political crisis is being resolved through legal channels. Anwar stressed that the situation did not stem from any failure within the state government, but rather from differing interpretations of the state constitution involving traditional institutions.

items.title: 31 nabbed in four ops as several scam syndicates crippled in Selangor
items.summary: Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said the operations, carried out by the Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) with assistance from district police headquarters, targeted call centre-style scam operations, online gambling fraud and investment scams. Police seized four laptops, 15 mobile phones, an iPad and a router, with total items valued at about RM20,000. Shazeli said in a second operation on April 20 about 6.45pm, police raided a unit at Brezza One Residency, Bukit Ampang Permai, and arrested 10 suspects — a local man and nine foreigners aged between 18 and 33.

items.title: Malaysia ultra-high-net-worth individuals to rise 39pc by 2031, says report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysia’s billionaire population is set to grow 39 per cent by 2031, according to Knight Frank’s flagship publication, The Wealth Report. It said Malaysia’s ultra-high-net-worth individuals (UHNWIs) are projected to showcase a five-year growth of 20.1 per cent, from 1,566 individuals in 2026 to 1,881 in 2031, representing a marked increase from the past five-year growth rate of 6.5 per cent (2021-2026). According to Knight Frank’s Prime International Residential Index, luxury residences in Kuala Lumpur showed a stable appreciation, growing by 1.1 per cent in 2025, in line with the residential segment’s steady performance in recent years.

items.title: Sabah to Putrajaya: Pay up the 40pc revenue now, we’ll keep fighting for bigger billions later
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 28 — Sabah has formally asked Putrajaya to immediately pay its 40 per cent revenue entitlement amounting to just over RM3 billion, with state Finance Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun insisting the claim is based on undisputed and publicly available tax data. Masidi said the state government wrote to the federal government on April 10 seeking payment of 40 per cent from two forms of taxes – customs and income tax – collected in Sabah which alone totalled RM7.547 billion in 2025. He said the state respects a Court of Appeal decision on April 6 to defer implementation of Sabah’s 40 per cent entitlement pending an appeal at the High Court in Kota Kinabalu, but emphasised that the decision relates specifically to retrospective claims.

items.title: Pantai Jerejak assemblyman's trial for allegedly hurting wife set for Aug 18
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: The magistrate's court here today fixed Aug 18 for the trial of Pantai Jerejak assemblyman Fahmi Zainol, who is charged with voluntarily causing hurt to his wife in February. Magistrate Chia Huey Ting also set May 14 for the submission of documents, including the medical report. "A further two-month postponement, even for mention, would only prolong the case unnecessarily," he told the court.

items.title: Will Negeri Sembilan crisis hit PKR-Umno ties elsewhere? Chang Lih Kang says too early to tell
items.summary: IPOH, April 28 — PKR vice-president Datuk Chang Lih Kang today urged caution following recent developments between PKR and Umno in Negeri Sembilan, saying the situation is still evolving and under close observation. This comes after Umno withdrew its support for the PKR-led menteri besar in Negeri Sembilan, raising questions about the stability of cooperation between the two parties elsewhere. Asked about the situation in Perak, Chang said there are currently no signs of similar tensions between PKR and Umno locally.

items.title: Selangor pork self-sufficiency drops to 10-15pc amid disease outbreak, says state exec
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Selangor’s self-sufficiency rate for pork has fallen sharply to about 10 to 15 per cent following a disease outbreak affecting livestock supply. State infrastructure and agriculture executive councillor Datuk Izham Hashim said the figure had previously stood at around 60 per cent, according to Berita Harian . The state is also monitoring supplies of frozen pork and carcass meat to ensure market demand is met, he said.

items.title: Sabah urged to tighten crocodile control measures along Kinabatangan River
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 28 — The Sabah state government has been urged to take proactive measures to manage the crocodile population in Sungai Kinabatangan and reduce risks to local residents, particularly fishermen. Mohd Ismail Ayob (BN-Lamag) proposed managing the crocodile population through relocation or culling in specific cases and permitting culling without a permit when absolutely necessary. The State Legislative Assembly sitting continues today.

items.title: Two Pahang women lose RM1.4m in phone scam syndicate fraud
items.summary: Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the first case involved a 60-year-old local woman who is a retired teacher in Kuantan. He said the victim received a phone call on September 6, 2024 from an individual posing as a police officer and informing her that she was allegedly involved in a money laundering case and the sale of ATM cards to criminals. “To deceive the victim, the suspect ordered the woman to make an affidavit and police report via phone call and recorded the victim’s voice as a confession.

items.title: Shamsul Iskandar’s bid to move graft trial to High Court rejected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s former senior political secretary Datuk Seri Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin has failed in his attempt to transfer his corruption case from the Sessions Court to the High Court. According to The Edge , Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin ruled on Tuesday that Shamsul had not met the legal threshold required to justify the transfer. Shamsul’s counsel Datuk Amer Hamzah Arshad and deputy public prosecutor Farah Ezlin Yusop Khan had previously made submissions on the matter earlier this month.

items.title: Former Rafizi aide James Chai arrives at MACC over RM1.11b semiconductor probe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 28 — Former aide to Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, Chai Jin Shern, also known as James Chai, arrived at the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) headquarters today to give his statement over a RM1.11 billion government-backed semiconductor project. According to the New Straits Times , Chai arrived at MACC headquarters at 9.59am. MACC later responded on March 13, granting an extension for him to appear on March 31.

items.title: Malaysia must build own AI capability, strengthen cybersecurity, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysia should not merely be a user of artificial intelligence (AI) technology, but needs to develop its own capabilities in the field, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said the country also needs to strengthen its AI ecosystem and enhance its competitiveness globally. Fahmi said he accompanied Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim to the launch of the Huawei AI Lab and Innovation Centre here yesterday.

items.title: Malaysia steps up measures to cushion impact of global supply crisis, says finance minister’s pol-sec
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The government remains committed to safeguarding the welfare of the people through various targeted subsidies and assistance, despite challenges arising from the global supply crisis and pressures on the country’s fiscal position. The Finance Minister’s political secretary, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, said the government is also strengthening strategies to diversify supply sources to avoid disruptions that could affect the national economy. Meanwhile, Muhammad Kamil said the government continues to enhance various forms of assistance, including Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA), as well as increasing targeted aid for those in need.

items.title: Early reforms help Malaysia weather West Asia crisis better than peers, says finance minister’s pol-sec
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Malaysia is weathering the fallout from the West Asia crisis better than many peers, supported by early government intervention over the past three years to strengthen economic resilience. Political secretary to the Finance Minister Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim said businesses, however, cannot operate as usual due to the crisis, whose implications extend beyond the domestic economy to the global stage. Muhammad Kamil said early interventions included targeted subsidy restructuring, fiscal discipline, a firm commitment to fighting corruption, and stronger financial management and governance.

items.title: Selangor govt demands swift action over online insult against Sultan Sharafuddin
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 28 — The Selangor government has called for swift and firm action by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) over a social media post insulting the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state’s stance had been conveyed to Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil during a meeting held yesterday afternoon. Elaborating, Amirudin stressed that the state government strongly condemns the irresponsible act, describing it as tarnishing and insulting the Selangor royal institution.

items.title: Surau on unapproved land in Ipoh demolished after court order, says police
items.summary: District police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the demolition was carried out at about 9.30 am after obtaining a court order from the Ipoh High Court, in accordance with legal provisions. He said also present during the operation were court bailiff, the housing developer, and representatives from the Perak Islamic Religious Department (JAIPk) and Perak Islamic Religious Council (MAIPk), to ensure the process was carried out in line with established procedures and regulations. “Overall, the demolition process proceeded smoothly without any untoward incidents,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Storm tears through Kedah, damages MRSM Pendang and several districts
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 28 — Strong storms and heavy winds swept across several districts in Kedah yesterday evening, damaging homes, government premises and schools, including Maktab Rendah Sains MARA (MRSM) Pendang. Pendang Civil Defence District officer Lt (PA) Yuslina Yusoff said the incident, which struck at about 6pm, affected several mukim including Padang Pusing, Ayer Putih and Rambai, as well as Kampung Gajah Mati, Paya Mat Inson and Kampung Lampam, reported national news agency Bernama. “Damage assessment and continuous monitoring were still underway,” she said.

items.title: Govt keeps BUDI95 petrol quota at 200 litres monthly amid supply concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — The government will maintain the temporary monthly quota for the BUDI MADANI RON95 initiative (BUDI95) at 200 litres for now amid the global supply crisis, said Finance Minister’s Political Secretary Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim. The government temporarily revised the BUDI95 quota from 300 litres to 200 litres a month effective April 1, 2026 following the conflict in West Asia, while the subsidised RON95 price remains at RM1.99 per litre. — Bernama

items.title: Anwar: N. Sembilan state election unlikely amid political uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said a state election in Negeri Sembilan is unlikely amid ongoing political uncertainty in the state. Earlier, the prime minister said any developments concerning the Negeri Sembilan state administration would be announced in due course. Meanwhile, Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said he would meet Anwar soon to discuss the matter.

items.title: ‘The rules will be made in Malaysia’: Anwar warns against AI becoming tool for Western influence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 28 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has warned that Artificial Intelligence (AI) and major technology firms risk being used as instruments of Western geopolitical dominance, rather than neutral commercial tools. Speaking at the launch of the Huawei AI Lab and Innovation Centre at The Exchange 106 in TRX, Anwar said parts of the Western tech industry view AI not just as a business sector, but as part of a broader competition for global supremacy. “We are confronted with some views, a very dogmatic doctrine that certain forms of governance, certain forms of technology, found in the West must dictate our policies,” he said.
